item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Where the Foundation puts up a sign saying "There be Monsters", this article takes a sharpie and scribbles "and Faeries and Unicorns and Elves and Satyrs and…"

I think this is the best description of the kind of style I tend to bring to the site with my articles and co-authored works. I love it!

Sorry this reply is so belated… Thank you very much for the comment and the +1. Cheers!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Writers on this site try to perpetuate 2 memes:

"There are things that go bump in the night, the GOC bumps back."

"There are monsters in your closet, the Foundation keeps them there"

But what if they aren't all monsters? What if the weirdness isn't evil? What if the weird is just weird?

There is one canon that explores this idea: Dixieland Nightmare Magic. However, this article shifts the core concept of this canon into the "prime" continuity.

Where the Foundation puts up a sign saying "There be Monsters", this article takes a sharpie and scribbles "and Faeries and Unicorns and Elves and Satyrs and…"

This is why you get your +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: With that additional knowledge in mind, this article becomes even more compelling! A really good read!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To tell you the truth, I'm thinking the exact same thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the positive comment!

Yeah the idea of kangaroos stealing cars is definitely a weird one, but hey, that's the foundation for you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of those that fits perfectly in my "The foundationverse runs on fairy tale logic" headcanon. X3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice work! Short sweet and to the point!

I do think however the words "Ransack" and "Hijack" are a bit off tone, but I think they work fine.

The idea of Kangaroos just freaking stealing a car kills me! Good execution!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the positive response! :)

What compels these marsupials to engage in this sort of behavior? Are they experiencing some sort of accelerated or advanced sapient inspiration, behaving like humans trying to fend for themselves in the wild?

Slight spoilers?
hide

All the SCP-XXXX-1 instances that hijack vehicles have been injured by vehicles before. They're trying to get their own back, by using the stolen vehicles for things like building shelter and growing food. The boosted intelligence and dexterity is part of the anomaly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Glad you liked the read, and thanks for the nice comment! :)

yeah, emus are scary. Cassowaries too. All these large boi birds. Large boirds?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Such a strange piece, but I must admit that I found it very intriguing!

It's short, but it definitely provokes the imagination of the reader. What compels these marsupials to engage in this sort of behavior? Are they experiencing some sort of accelerated or advanced sapient inspiration, behaving like humans trying to fend for themselves in the wild?

Great article!

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
ETA 2: After consideration, the page is now (properly!) listed as a co-authored work!

ETA: as of 06/21/18, TheManhattenProject has requested that this no longer be listed as a co-authorship, due to the imbalance of writing contribution. As I wanted to have their name still associated with this article rather than taking all the credit, I will list it as a rewrite instead.

This was pretty fun to write! Started off as an ideas thread in the forums, went to a draft thread, then to chat. We ran the entire circuit.

It was great working with TheManhattenProject/Pratten(chat nick), and an interesting ride looking through all sorts of information on kangaroos. The small town mentioned is Yaraka, by the way. Also the video of the kangaroo sitting in a truck and beeping the horn is amazing, go watch it.

We hope you enjoyed the read!

For those interested in the co-authoring process, there's a new page up with some information and instructions for new and experienced authors alike on how to co-author pages.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dig it.

Short and quirky, and packing a decent punch during its brief stay (pun intended).

Hopefully the Foundation's efforts to contain this go better than that other time the wildlife in Australia caused massive problems. Damn emus.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
First of all, a huge thank you to the Zyn for Co-authoring the article with me, couldn't of done it without her! Also a big thank you to those to helped and reviewed our work: Golden506, CaptainKirby, and Apoplexic, thank you so much!

Image credit goes to: https://pixabay.com/en/australia-kangaroo-road-shield-2282288/ (licensed under CC0 Creative Commons)

Also, if you would like more info on this, please visit here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=S7PEdAdvq3g
